name: telegram_api_usage_audit

analysis.notifications_py: Uses contextlib.suppress(Exception) intentionally to prevent notification failures from breaking main flow. This is acceptable but errors are not logged.
analysis.publication_service_delete: Uses suppress(TelegramBadRequest) correctly for delete_message — not an issue.

issues.description: send_message() in middleware without try/except — may fail if user blocked bot
issues.impact: Middleware may crash, blocking request processing

issues.description: send_message() in FSM middleware without error handling
issues.impact: May fail silently or crash middleware

issues.description: send_message() in throttle middleware without error handling
issues.impact: May fail silently or crash middleware

issues.description: get_chat_member() without try/except — will fail if bot not in chat
issues.impact: Publication service will crash if channel removed bot

false_positives.reason: All notification functions use contextlib.suppress(Exception) to handle Telegram errors gracefully. This is intentional design to prevent notification failures from breaking main flow.
delete_message() uses 'with suppress(TelegramBadRequest)' — correct error handling for already-deleted messages

notes: 13 FALSE POSITIVES: notifications.py uses contextlib.suppress(Exception) intentionally
1 FALSE POSITIVE: publication_service.py delete_message uses suppress(TelegramBadRequest)
5 VALID HIGH issues: middleware files and publication_service.get_chat_member lack error handling
Recommendation: Add try/except with logging to middleware send_message calls
Recommendation: Add try/except to get_chat_member to handle 'bot kicked' scenario
